Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add comprehensive transformations reference to API mapping

Added detailed documentation for all Shopify → Exasol transformations:
- GID extraction \(extract numeric from gid://shopify/Type/ID\)
- Date/Time key derivation from ISO timestamps
- Money extraction from MoneyBag objects
- Array to string conversion for tags
- Boolean derivation logic \(is_fulfilled, is_refunded, etc.\)
- Address hashing for dim_geography deduplication
- Null handling with field-specific defaults
- Marketing state mapping \(SUBSCRIBED → TRUE\)
- Discount type mapping \(__typename → friendly code\)
- Surrogate key generation strategy
- Tax line aggregation
- Variant options extraction

Updated field mapping tables to show transformation function for each field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add GraphQL vs REST clarification to API mapping

Added "Understanding GraphQL Queries" section explaining:
- Single endpoint vs multiple REST endpoints
- Query body contains the "query" \(orders, products, etc.\)
- Visual diagram showing POST body structure
- Comparison table: REST endpoints vs GraphQL queries

Improves documentation clarity for readers familiar with REST APIs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add two-layer schema design with STG and DWH definitions

New comprehensive schema document \(schema-layered.md\) includes:

STAGING SCHEMA \(SHOPIFY_STG\):
- 10 tables mirroring Shopify API structure
- stg_orders, stg_order_line_items, stg_order_transactions
- stg_order_tax_lines, stg_order_discount_applications
- stg_order_shipping_lines, stg_customers, stg_products
- stg_product_variants, stg_discount_codes, stg_locations

DATA WAREHOUSE SCHEMA \(SHOPIFY_DWH\):
- Reporting-optimized with pivoted arrays to columns
- fact_order: 70+ columns with pivoted payments, taxes, discounts
- fact_order_line_item: denormalized product/customer/geography
- dim_customer: includes LTV metrics and segmentation
- dim_product, dim_geography, dim_discount, dim_location
- dim_date, dim_time \(generated\)

DATA LINEAGE:
- Visual diagram showing API → STG → DWH flow
- Transformation summary by table
- Pivot transformation examples with SQL
- Column count comparison \(STG vs DWH\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add layered schema Word doc and update tracking/memory

Documentation:
- Created Shopify-DWH-Schema-Layered.docx for design review
- Updated tasks.md with completed work \(schema redesign, API mapping enhancements\)
- Refined ETL backlog with STG + DWH layer specifics

Memory:
- Created episodic: 2026-01-30-schema-layered-design.md
- Added patterns: Two-Layer DWH Architecture, Pivot Transformation
- Updated pattern index and review log

Key learning: Design DWH for reporting from the start, not as
a "clean" version of the source that then gets denormalized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Extract learnings from architecture decisions session

/learn session captured:
- New pattern: Architecture Selection \(Warehouse vs Lakehouse\)
- Episodic: 2026-01-30-architecture-decisions.md
- Updated brain-health metrics with actual counts

Key insights:
1. DWH should be reporting-optimized from the start
2. Lakehouse wasn''t needed \(no unstructured data, no ML\)
3. Document transformations upfront
4. Design for consumer needs, not source structure

Brain stats: 19 patterns, 10 episodic entries, Week 1 goals exceeded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "C:\\Users\\dominica\\source\\repos\\Shopify-DWH" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add productization strategy for configuration-driven deployments

Created productization-strategy.md covering:
- Configuration schema \(deployment_config.yaml\)
- Dynamic DDL generation \(payment_shopify_payments_amount vs payment_1_gateway\)
- Parameterized ETL pivot logic
- Auto-discovery from Shopify stores
- Deployment workflow for new customers
- Implementation phases

Key insight: Named columns per payment method/tax type/discount category
instead of generic numbered columns \(payment_1, payment_2\).

Updated:
- context.md with new documentation links
- tasks.md with productization backlog items

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
